title: gem5 Architecture Simulation Series
summary: Implement a series of 4 papers advancing architecture simulation, building on a Python-based event-driven simulation framework. The base gem5 simulator provides CPU models and classic memory system, extended by gem5 v20+ (stdlib components, multi-level caches, branch prediction), gem5-Aladdin (accelerator co-design with DMA and cache interfaces), and gem5-SALAM (LLVM-based accelerator modeling with instruction scheduling).
instructionPreview: Implement a gem5-inspired architecture simulation framework in Python.
instruction:
Implement a gem5-inspired architecture simulation framework in Python.

Working directory: /workspace
The base framework provides an event-driven simulation engine (sim_engine.py)
and deterministic workloads (workloads.py).

Implement the following modules in topological order:

1. gem5 (The gem5 Simulator): Full CPU models including AtomicSimpleCPU (single-cycle),
   TimingSimpleCPU (memory timing, branch penalties), and O3CPU (out-of-order
   superscalar with fetch/decode/rename/dispatch/issue/execute/commit pipeline
   stages, ROB, LSQ, physical register file, BTB, RAS, TLB). Classic memory
   system with Cache (MSHR queue, write buffer, MOESI coherence protocol, LRU),
   detailed DRAMController (ranks/banks/row buffers, timing: tCAS/tRCD/tRP/tRAS,
   address mapping, refresh scheduling, FR-FCFS request queue), SE-mode runner.
   Files: cpu_models.py, memory_system.py, se_runner.py

2. gem5_v20 (Version 20.0+): Stdlib components API (Board, Processor,
   CacheHierarchy), multi-level L1/L2 cache, TAGE branch predictor (with loop
   predictor, statistical corrector, indirect predictor, perceptron predictor),
   HBM2Controller (pseudo-channels, bank groups, bandwidth model),
   NVMController (read/write asymmetry, wear leveling, write buffer),
   hierarchical StatSystem, CheckpointManager, full-system support
   (device tree generation, GIC interrupt controller).
   Files: stdlib_components.py, cache_hierarchy.py, branch_predictor.py

3. gem5_aladdin (Accelerator Co-Design): DDDGBuilder (dynamic data dependence
   graph from execution traces), DataflowScheduler (ASAP/ALAP/list scheduling),
   LoopAnalyzer (unroll factor, pipeline II), AcceleratorDatapath, DMA engine
   (bulk transfer, gather/scatter), PipelinedDMA, DMATriggeredCompute,
   Scratchpad (multi-port, bandwidth model), CacheBasedAccelerator,
   AcceleratorSimObject (MMIO control registers), PowerModel (per-op energy,
   leakage), SoC interconnect, memory trace analyzer, EDP analysis.
   Files: accelerator.py, dma_optimizations.py, accel_runner.py

4. gem5_salam (LLVM-based Accelerator): Full IR parser (all instruction types:
   arith/cmp/mem/control/cast), CDFGBuilder (control+data flow graph, dominator
   tree, loop back-edge detection), LivenessAnalysis, ResourceLibrary (FU types
   with latency/area/power), ASAPScheduler, ALAPScheduler, ListScheduler,
   PipelineScheduler (modulo scheduling, MII computation), FunctionalUnit model,
   MMIOInterface (register map for CPU control), CycleSimulator (per-cycle FU
   occupation), HW accelerator cluster with SPM and DMA.
   Files: llvm_interface.py, scheduler.py, hw_accelerator.py

Each module updates run.sh to output experiment results as EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE.
See requirements/ for detailed specifications of each module.

moduleDag.description: Implement a series of 4 papers advancing architecture simulation, building on a Python-based event-driven simulation framework. The base gem5 simulator provides CPU models and classic memory system, extended by gem5 v20+ (stdlib components, multi-level caches, branch prediction), gem5-Aladdin (accelerator co-design with DMA and cache interfaces), and gem5-SALAM (LLVM-based accelerator modeling with instruction scheduling).

nodes.label: The gem5 Simulator
nodes.description: Implements the core gem5 simulator: AtomicSimple, TimingSimple, and O3 (out-of-order) CPU models with full pipeline stages (fetch/decode/rename/dispatch/issue/execute/commit), ROB, LSQ, physical register file. Classic memory system with cache (MSHR, write buffer, MOESI coherence protocol), detailed DRAM controller (ranks/banks/row buffers/timing), address mapper, TLB, BTB, RAS, and SE-mode simulation runner.

nodes.label: The gem5 Simulator: Version 20.0+
nodes.description: Adds v20+ improvements: component-based stdlib API (Board, Processor, CacheHierarchy), multi-level L1/L2 cache hierarchy, TAGE branch predictor with loop predictor and statistical corrector, perceptron predictor, HBM2 controller (pseudo-channels, bank groups), NVM controller (read/write asymmetry, wear leveling), hierarchical statistics system, checkpoint manager, full-system support (device tree, GIC interrupt controller).

nodes.label: Co-Designing Accelerators and SoC Interfaces using gem5-Aladdin
nodes.description: Implements accelerator co-design: DDDG construction from execution traces, dataflow scheduler (ASAP/ALAP/list scheduling), loop analyzer (unroll/pipeline II), DMA engine with gather/scatter, pipelined DMA, DMA-triggered compute, scratchpad with multi-port bandwidth model, cache-based accelerator, AcceleratorSimObject with MMIO control registers, power model (per-op energy, leakage), SoC interconnect, accelerator cluster, memory trace analyzer, and EDP analysis.

nodes.label: gem5-SALAM: LLVM-based Accelerator Modeling
nodes.description: Adds LLVM-based accelerator modeling: full IR parser supporting all instruction types (arithmetic/comparison/memory/control/cast), CDFG builder (control+data flow graph with dominator tree and loop detection), liveness analysis, resource library (FU types with latency/area/power), ASAP/ALAP/list schedulers, pipeline (modulo) scheduler with MII computation, MMIO interface for CPU-accelerator communication, cycle-accurate simulator with FU occupation tracking, and HW accelerator cluster.

edges.label: v20+ builds on base gem5 simulator
Aladdin extends gem5 with accelerator co-design
SALAM extends gem5 with LLVM-based accelerator modeling
